text.title: Competitor Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Competitor Analysis methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Identify all three competitor types: direct (5-10), indirect (3-5), future (2-3) with concrete signals.
rule.rationale: Direct-only analysis misses substitutes customers actually use and entrants arriving in 18 months.

rule.statement: Plot every competitor on a 2x2 matrix (price vs feature-breadth) before selecting your own positioning.
rule.rationale: Visual mapping surfaces whitespace that a feature list alone obscures.

rule.statement: Analyse each competitor across all 8 dimensions; do not skip dimensions.
rule.rationale: Partial analysis produces a partial gap table; investors probe the dimensions you skipped.

rule.statement: Differentiate on a competitor weakness; scrape 1-star reviews and convert top-3 complaints into your positioning.
rule.rationale: Copying competitors produces table-stakes; complaints-as-headline resonate with dissatisfied users.

rule.statement: Cite a URL for every factual cell (price, founding year, employee count); reject cells without sources.
rule.rationale: Sourced claims are auditable; unsourced cells are liabilities.

rule.statement: Run per-competitor snapshot tasks in parallel; run synthesis serially.
rule.rationale: Dumping 10+ competitors into one prompt overflows context and degrades gap analysis.

rule.statement: Pin target geography in every prompt; reject competitors whose primary market differs.
rule.rationale: Without geo pinning, agents skew toward US companies even for EU/APAC markets.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
